 {"id":"gt-iu23","title":"Polecat doesn't auto-start after spawn inject - requires manual nudge","description":"## Problem

After `gt spawn --issue <id> --create`, the polecat session shows Claude Code started with the injected prompt, but Claude doesn't begin processing. The prompt just sits there until manually nudged.

## Evidence

```
$ gt spawn --issue gt-rixa --rig gastown --create
...
✓ Session started. Attach with: gt session at gastown/furiosa
  Polecat nudged to start working
  Witness notified to monitor startup
```

Session shows:
```
> You have a work assignment. Run 'gt mail inbox' to see it, then start
  working on issue gt-rixa.

  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
```

But Claude doesn't respond. Manual nudge required:
```
$ gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa "Please start working..."
```

After nudge, polecat immediately starts working correctly.

## Hypothesis

The spawn inject happens before Claude Code is fully initialized. The text arrives in the input buffer, but Claude hasn't started listening yet. By the time Claude starts, the input has already been 'consumed' as initial prompt text but not submitted.

## Resolution Plan

This will be solved by the **polecat molecule workflow** (mol-polecat-work), which provides structured lifecycle management. The molecule approach handles startup, work, and shutdown as discrete steps with proper state tracking.

**Blocked on**: beads/crew/dave completing ephemeral molecules (bd mol bond, ephemeral beads repo).

## Workaround

For now, use `gt nudge` if a polecat doesn't start within ~30 seconds of spawn.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:06:27.375686-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:53:46.940942-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-j5tk","title":"Work assignment messages should auto-close on completion","description":"When a polecat completes work on an issue, the work assignment message (msg-type:task) stays open. Found 7 stale work assignments in gastown after swarm completed.

Proposal: When bd close is called on an issue, auto-close any work assignment messages that reference that issue in their body.

Alternative: Work assignment messages could use a different lifecycle - perhaps they should be acked (closed) when the polecat starts working, not when they finish.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:12:28.403974-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T21:00:15.945049-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T21:00:15.945049-08:00","close_reason":"Nice-to-have polish features. +{"id":"gt-ne41f","title":"Hardcoded 'gastown' rig in Deacon patrol context","description":"In prime.go, the Deacon patrol context hardcodes 'gastown' as the rig name.

**Location**: prime.go L807

```go
rigBeadsDir := filepath.Join(ctx.TownRoot, "gastown", "mayor", "rig")
```

This breaks if:
- The rig is renamed
- The town has multiple rigs
- The Deacon is deployed to a different rig

**Fix options**:
1. Detect rig from context (ctx.Rig)
2. Use town-level beads for Deacon patrol
3. Pass rig name as parameter

The Deacon is a town-level role, so option 2 may be most appropriate.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T22:03:12.131973-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T22:03:12.131973-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-ng6g","title":"Implement await type handlers (gh:run, gh:pr, timer, human, mail)","description":"Implement condition checking for each await type.

## Handlers Needed
- gh:run:<id> - Check GitHub Actions run status via gh CLI
- gh:pr:<id> - Check PR merged/closed status via gh CLI  
- timer:<duration> - Simple elapsed time check
- human:<prompt> - Check for human approval (via mail?)
- mail:<pattern> - Check for mail matching pattern

## Interface
```go
type AwaitHandler interface {
    Check(awaitID string) (completed bool, result string, err error)
}
```

## Moved from beads
Originally bd-2l03. Deacon handlers belong in gastown.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:12.427881-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:12.427881-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ng6g","depends_on_id":"gt-dh65","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:32.734889-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

+{"id":"gt-sq0t3","title":"BUG: gt handoff --dry-run still sends mail","description":"In handoff.go, the dry-run check happens AFTER sendHandoffMail() is called. This means --dry-run still sends mail.

**Location**: handoff.go L126-143

**Observed**: Testing with `gt handoff --dry-run -s 'Test' -m 'Message'` actually sent mail to mayor.

**Fix**: Move mail sending inside the non-dry-run block, or check dry-run first.

```go
// Current (buggy):
if handoffSubject \!= "" || handoffMessage \!= "" {
    sendHandoffMail(...)  // Executes even in dry-run\!
}
if handoffDryRun {        // Too late
    return nil
}

// Should be:
if handoffDryRun {
    // show what would happen
    return nil
}
// Then send mail
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T22:02:51.953925-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T22:02:51.953925-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-zivp","title":"mol-outpost-assign: Intelligent work routing to outposts","description":"The federation design shows outposts.yaml with a static policy section. Simple preference ordering is fine as config, but intelligent work routing should be a molecule.

Current static approach:
```yaml
policy:
  default_preference: [local, gce-burst, cloudrun-burst]
```

Cases requiring cognition:
- "This is a long-running research task, route to VM not CloudRun"
- "This touches sensitive code, keep local"
- "These 5 issues are related, batch them to same outpost"
- "CloudRun cost is high today, prefer local even if slower"

## Molecule: outpost-assign
Intelligent work-to-outpost routing.

## Step: classify-work
Analyze the issue/work item:
- Expected duration (quick fix vs multi-hour)
- Resource requirements
- Sensitivity/security tier
- Related work (same epic?)

## Step: check-capacity
Query outpost status:
- Current load on each
- Cost accrued today
- Health status

## Step: select-outpost
Choose optimal outpost based on:
- Work classification
- Capacity/cost
- Policy constraints
Needs: classify-work, check-capacity

## Step: emit-assignment
Record decision in beads for audit.
Needs: select-outpost

## Notes
- This molecule is invoked by Mayor/Witness when spawning
- Simple cases can short-circuit to static policy
- Full analysis only for ambiguous cases","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:17.964834-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:17.964834-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-zko","title":"gt rig info: Show detailed rig information","description":"Add 'gt rig info <rig>' command to show detailed rig status.

Should show:
- Rig path and git URL

- Refinery status
- Witness status
- Recent activity
- Beads summary (open issues count)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:47:17.879255-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zko","depends_on_id":"gt-hw6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:22:47.502099-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
+{"id":"gt-zlro5","title":"Refactor patrol context functions to reduce duplication","description":"In prime.go, three patrol context functions are nearly identical (~100 lines each):
- outputDeaconPatrolContext() - L797-940
- outputWitnessPatrolContext() - L942-1106
- outputRefineryPatrolContext() - L1108-1272

They all:
1. Check for existing patrol molecule
2. Auto-spawn if none exists
3. Display patrol work loop instructions

**Suggested refactor**:
Extract common logic into a helper:
```go
func outputPatrolContext(ctx RoleContext, patrolName string, assignee string) {
    // shared logic
}
```

Then each role-specific function just calls the helper with appropriate parameters.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T22:03:13.712525-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T22:03:13.712525-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-zly","title":"Swarm learning: Beads database locality gap","description":"## PGT Bug (GGT Design Already Correct)

Mayor created issues in `~/ai/mayor/rigs/beads/.beads/` but polecats use `~/ai/beads/.beads/`. Different databases = polecats can't see Mayor's beads.

**GGT Fix**: architecture.md already specifies:
- All agents use BEADS_DIR pointing to rig-level `.beads/`
- Lines 116-143: Beads Configuration for Multi-Agent
- Lines 573-586: Rig-Level Beads via BEADS_DIR

This is a PGT implementation gap, not a design issue. GGT spawn must set BEADS_DIR correctly.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:21:45.37072-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-zn35j","title":"Nested loop support in control flow","description":"Currently, nested loops (a loop inside a loop body) are not supported.

In expandLoopIteration(), the Loop field is intentionally not copied:
```go
// Note: Loop field intentionally not copied - nested loops need explicit support
```

To support nested loops:
1. After expanding the outer loop iteration, recursively call ApplyLoops on the body steps
2. Handle nested iteration ID prefixing (e.g., `outer.iter1.inner.iter2.step`)
3. Add tests for nested loop scenarios

This is a lower-priority enhancement since most real-world formulas use single-level loops.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:13:49.880102-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T16:23:05.402922-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T16:23:05.402922-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented nested loop support with recursive ApplyLoops call.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zn35j","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T15:14:18.859367-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
